sections.paragraph: m. progeny, as well rendered by the Vulg., always joined with the syn. נִין which see. With this accords Eth. ነገድ፡ race, kindred, tribe (כ and ג being interchanged, compare נגד for נכד Gen 21:23, in the Samaritan copy.-As to Job 31:3, where in some MSS. and editions there is נֶכֶד (for the common נֶכֶר), it may be i.q. Arab. نَكْدُ wretched life; but the common reading is to be preferred.
